items.title: How Do I Start Mining at Home?
items.description: Home mining has three entry points: low-power hardware (Bitaxe), professional ASICs at home (S19, S21), or rented industrial hashrate you control via template protocols (DATUM/Stratum V2). This page maps all three paths from first purchase to first accepted share.

items.title: Mining Decentralization
items.description: Proof-of-Work is what makes Bitcoin immutable. At Meetup #2 and again in January 2026 we covered the mechanics: miners hash block data, race for a valid nonce, and broadcast solutions that the network accepts. The difficulty adjustment targets ten minutes per block. The supply cap is twenty-one million. None of this works without distributed hash rate competing to find the next block.

items.title: Mining Payout Schemas
items.description: Mining pools and solo setups distribute block rewards through different payout schemas. Each schema trades off variance (how predictable your income is), fees (what the operator charges), transparency (how rewards are calculated), and sovereignty (how much control you retain over block templates).

items.title: Mining with Rented Hashrate: Braiins Hashpower + DATUM
items.description: Pared-down outline for ~45 min: 20 min content + 25 min demos & Q&A.

items.title: Ocean.xyz
items.description: Ocean.xyz is a Bitcoin mining pool that emphasizes transparency, non-custodial payouts, and miner sovereignty through the DATUM protocol.
